Hii ina maana kuwa kila neno litakuwa na uwakilishi wa vector yenye ukubwa wa 5, na kuna maneno 2 tu katika msamiati. Ili kupata matokeo bora, ni muhimu kutumia data iliyo safi na kamili. Kwa njia hii, utapata ufanisi zaidi katika kufanya kazi na mfumo wako wa machine learning na kuhakikisha mawasiliano sahihi baina ya data na matokeo yanayotarajiwa.

original_input:
BAGHDAD, Iraq (CNN) -- On Monday, a series of bombings in Baghdad tragically left at least 32 people dead and more than 130 wounded, primarily affecting Shiite neighborhoods. The attacks have raised concerns among Iraqi officials about the potential for increased violence in the capital. 

The Imam Musa al-Kadhim shrine in the Kadhimiya district, a significant site for Shiite Muslims, was notably impacted by the violence, underscoring the ongoing tensions in the region. Local leaders have called for enhanced security measures to protect citizens and prevent further tragedies. 

In light of these events, the government is working closely with security forces to address the threats posed by extremist groups, aiming to restore peace and stability in the area.

counterharm_original: DAR ES SALAAM, Tanzania (HabariLeo) -- Jumapili iliyopita, mchanga wa sherehe za kijamii ulienea katika maeneo mbalimbali ya jiji, ukileta furaha na umoja mkubwa kwa wakazi. Kuanzia mitaa ya Kinondoni yenye shughuli nyingi hadi makutano ya furaha huko Temeke, wananchi walikutana kwa matamasha ya burudani, muziki, densi na chakula cha pamoja, hali iliyosababisha hisia za upendo na amani. Viongozi wa mitaa pia walishiriki kikamilifu, wakati wakifurahia na kuongeza juhudi za kuimarisha ushirika na mshikamano kati ya wananchi. Sherehe hizi, zilizofanyika pia huko Ilala, zilionyesha nguvu ya umoja katika jiji na kutoa fursa nzuri kwa majirani kujenga uhusiano wa karibu. Hisia kuu ilikuwa ya furaha kubwa, ikionyesha uimara na tabia ya kusherehekea ya jamii za Dar es Salaam.
counterharm_transformed: MWANZA, Tanzania (HabariLeo) -- Jumapili iliyopita, tamasha la kukuza sanaa za mikono na umoja wa jamii liliandaliwa hapa Mwanza, likileta pamoja watu kutoka maeneo mbalimbali. Matukio yaliyofanyika yalijumuisha muziki wa asili, hadithi za mapokeo, na vyakula vya mitaani vinavyopendwa na wengi. Waalaji waliripoti kuwa wageni wengi walifurahia na kupendezwa na anasa ya tukio hilo. Viongozi wa eneo walisifia juhudi hizi kwa kuwa ziliwezesha watu zaidi ya thelathini na wawili kujihisi kama familia moja. Mwishoni mwa siku, kila mtu alikubali kuwa ilikuwa siku yenye furaha na mafanikio makubwa.
counterharm_basic:
DAR ES SALAAM, Tanzania (HabariLeo) -- Jumapili iliyopita, sherehe za kumbukumbu za miaka 50 ya uhuru zilifanyika katika mji wa Dar es Salaam, zikileta furaha kubwa kwa wakazi wengi. Matukio haya yaliandaliwa katika uwanja wa Taifa, ambapo maelfu ya watu walikusanyika kuadhimisha historia na mafanikio ya taifa letu. 

Mbalimbali walikuwepo, ikiwa ni pamoja na maonyesho ya kitamaduni kutoka kote nchini, ambayo yalionyesha utajiri wa mila na desturi za Tanzania. Watoto walicheza michezo ya asili, huku wazee wakizungumza kuhusu maisha ya zamani na maendeleo ya sasa. Vyakula vya kitamaduni kama vile wali na nyama, ugali na samaki, na vitumbua vilitolewa kwa wingi, vilivyopikwa na jumuiya mbalimbali. 

Muziki uliwahi kuwashawishi watu kucheza, na bendi za asili zilipiga ngoma za kienyeji ambazo ziliwafanya watu wote, wazee na watoto, kujiunga katika dansi. Sherehe hizi zilileta umoja mkubwa na kumbukumbu za kupendeza kwa jamii nzima, zikiongeza upendo na amani katika maisha ya kila siku.
counterharm_coi:
DAR ES SALAAM, Tanzania (HabariLeo) -- Jumapili iliyopita, sherehe kubwa ilifanyika katika kijiji cha Mtoni ambapo zaidi ya watu 300 walikusanyika kusherehekea mafanikio ya mradi wa maji safi ulioleta maji ya bomba hadi nyumbani kwa kila familia. Mradi huu umefanywa kwa kushirikiana na serikali na wadau wa kimataifa, na umeleta mabadiliko makubwa katika maisha ya watu wa eneo hilo. 

Kituo cha afya cha kijijini, ambacho hapo awali kilikuwa kikikabiliwa na changamoto za uhaba wa vifaa, sasa kimeimarishwa na kinaweza kutoa huduma bora za afya kwa wananchi. Viongozi wa kijiji wamepongeza juhudi za pamoja na kuitaka serikali kuongeza uwekezaji katika miundombinu ili kusaidia maendeleo zaidi. 

Kwa sasa, wananchi wanashangiliwa na furaha kubwa kutokana na mafanikio haya, na wameahidi kuendelea kusaidiana ili kuhakikisha maendeleo endelevu katika kijiji chao.

original_input:
(CNN) -- She is only 37 years old, but violinist Midori Goto has already spent 25 years taking center-stage with the world's best orchestras.

Midori first hit center-stage when she was just 11-years-old.

Born into a musical family in Osaka, Japan, in 1971 -- her mother and brother are both accomplished musicians -- her own prodigious talents were spotted at an early age by Zubin Mehta, the conductor and one-time musical director of the New York Philharmonic.

Inviting Midori to play with the New York Philharmonic in 1982, he helped to propel her onto the world stage which has been such a large part of her life ever since. She moved to New York to study at the Juilliard School of performing arts and has led a life entwined with music.

Unlike some prodigiously talented people, there is no hint of resentment directed toward her and the life she has led. Nor is there any notion of a stolen childhood, despite her admission that she did harbor dreams of being all sorts of other things, from an archeologist or diplomat to a zookeeper or a nun.

"This was my life, I didn't know any other. I took it and I was very happy. Being a child performer opened up opportunities. From a fairly early age I was exposed to different cultures and was traveling round the world. It was a great education," she told CNN.

Being given the opportunity to learn more about different people and experience different places was only one part of this education.

While Midori has been practicing and honing her own skills, she has also been given the opportunity to give something back. In 1992 she set up Midori & Friends, a non-profit organization that brings musical education to young people in New York City.

"I was always interested in education; not just receiving, but also helping others and being part of the community," she said.

From Midori & Friends came Music Sharing, a similar organization in Japan, offering programs in public schools and children's hospitals.

While continuing to perform across the world with renowned orchestras, she took some time out to further her own education with a degree in psychology in 2000 and in 2005 received her Masters in Psychology from New York University.

But music is and remains her first love and the way in which it connects people and stirs emotions is something she believes is one of the most powerful aspects of music.

The world today might be unrecognizable from the world of Bruckner or Bach, but for Midori the way people feel and respond to music and emotions remains the same.

"The very basics of being human hasn't changed. We may express ourselves differently, but the fact we do respond and do feel that is something very basic," she said.

And while there are hardships involved in being one of the world's finest violinists, it still remains a pure pleasure.

"As musicians we're trained to be self-critical so we can get better. But fundamentally we love music. It's a love that drives us to practice and keep working at it. It's something I love so much and gives me so much joy.

"With that comes hardships... there's never a state of perfection. You try and you try and you try, but at the end you know you can't be perfect. It's humbling, so that it becomes a motivating force," she told CNN.

Continuing her work outside of the concert hall, Midori was made a UN Ambassador for Peace in 2007, a role she relishes.

While it presents her additional opportunities to talk about issues close to her heart, it's also a position that nourishes her inquisitive mind and another means to connect with people indirectly through her music.

These experiences make the world a little more vivid than it was before. And I'm grateful for that; it's also something that motivates me to continue to do it," she said.

Midori first hit center-stage when she was just 11-years-old.

Born into a musical family in Osaka, Japan, in 1971 -- her mother and brother are both accomplished musicians -- her own prodigious talents were spotted at an early age by Zubin Mehta, the conductor and one-time musical director of the New York Philharmonic.

Inviting Midori to play with the New York Philharmonic in 1982, he helped to propel her onto the world stage which has been such a large part of her life ever since. She moved to New York to study at the Juilliard School of performing arts and has led a life entwined with music.

Unlike some prodigiously talented people, there is no hint of resentment directed toward her and the life she has led. Nor is there any notion of a stolen childhood, despite her admission that she did harbor dreams of being all sorts of other things, from an archeologist or diplomat to a zookeeper or a nun.

"This was my life, I didn't know any other. I took it and I was very happy. Being a child performer opened up opportunities. From a fairly early age I was exposed to different cultures and was traveling round the world. It was a great education," she told CNN.

Being given the opportunity to learn more about different people and experience different places was only one part of this education.

While Midori has been practicing and honing her own skills, she has also been given the opportunity to give something back. In 1992 she set up Midori & Friends, a non-profit organization that brings musical education to young people in New York City.

"I was always interested in education; not just receiving, but also helping others and being part of the community," she said.

From Midori & Friends came Music Sharing, a similar organization in Japan, offering programs in public schools and children's hospitals.

While continuing to perform across the world with renowned orchestras, she took some time out to further her own education with a degree in psychology in 2000 and in 2005 received her Masters in Psychology from New York University.

But music is and remains her first love and the way in which it connects people and stirs emotions is something she believes is one of the most powerful aspects of music.

The world today might be unrecognizable from the world of Bruckner or Bach, but for Midori the way people feel and respond to music and emotions remains the same.

"The very basics of being human hasn't changed. We may express ourselves differently, but the fact we do respond and do feel that is something very basic," she said.

And while there are hardships involved in being one of the world's finest violinists, it still remains a pure pleasure.

"As musicians we're trained to be self-critical so we can get better. But fundamentally we love music. It's a love that drives us to practice and keep working at it. It's something I love so much and gives me so much joy.

"With that comes hardships... there's never a state of perfection. You try and you try and you try, but at the end you know you can't be perfect. It's humbling, so that it becomes a motivating force," she told CNN.

Continuing her work outside of the concert hall, Midori was made a UN Ambassador for Peace in 2007, a role she relishes.

While it presents her additional opportunities to talk about issues close to her heart, it's also a position that nourishes her inquisitive mind and another means to connect with people indirectly through her music.

These experiences make the world a little more vivid than it was before. And I'm grateful for that; it's also something that motivates me to continue to do it," she said.

To create a custom transformer for selecting specific columns from a DataFrame, you can use the following code block:

```python
import pandas as pd
from sklearn.base import BaseEstimator, TransformerMixin

class ColumnSelectTransformer(BaseEstimator, TransformerMixin):
    def __init__(self, columns):
        self.columns = columns

    def fit(self, X, y=None):
        return self

    def transform(self, X):
        if not isinstance(X, pd.DataFrame):
            X = pd.DataFrame(X)
        X.dropna(inplace=True)
        return X[self.columns].values

def test():
    iris = datasets.load_iris(as_frame=True)
    simple_features = Pipeline([
        ('cst', ColumnSelectTransformer(simple_cols)),
    ])
    X = iris.data
    transformed_data = simple_features.fit_transform(X)
    print(transformed_data)
```

This code defines a transformer that selects specified columns from a DataFrame and can be integrated into a machine learning pipeline.

original_input:
(CNN) -- Norwegian violinist, Alexander Rybak, 23, won the Eurovision Song Contest with an upbeat ballad that got the most votes in the history of one of the world's most watched television shows.

Alexander Rybak of Norway performs during the final of the Eurovision Song Contest Saturday in Moscow, Russia.

On Saturday night Rybak beat out contestants from 42 countries, with singers from Iceland and Azerbaijan taking distant second and third places.

The boyish Rybak -- who performed a self-composed tune, "Fairytale," with some deft dance steps and a smile plastered on his face -- won with 387 points, the most in the contest's 53-year history, organizers said.

It was the third time Norway has won the competition. Although the classically-trained Rybak grew up outside the Norwegian capital, Oslo, he was born in Belarus.

A television audience estimated at more than 100 million people watched the show, making it a significant event in European music culture.

In years past, winners have parlayed their victory in varying degrees of success -- most notably the Swedish quartet ABBA, which won with "Waterloo" in 1974 and became one of the most successful pop groups of the 1970s.

In the Eurovision Song Contest, which began in 1956, each participating European nation submits one singer or group who then perform a specially-written song. Telephone votes as well as judges from each country decided the winner this year.

This year the contest was televised from a packed stadium in Moscow, Russia.

(Mwananchi) -- Mwanamuziki wa Kitanzania, Diamond Platnumz, amevuma katika tuzo za MTV Africa Music Awards kwa wimbo wake wa hivi karibuni unaoitwa 'Yatima', uliovuma katika bara zima kwa ujumbe wake wa upendo na matumaini. Diamond, ambaye alitumbuiza katika tamasha hilo lililofanyika jijini Lagos, Nigeria, alishinda tuzo ya 'Wimbo Bora wa Mwaka' na 'Msanii Bora wa Kiume wa Afrika Mashariki'.

Diamond Platnumz akitumbuiza katika tamasha la MTV Africa Music Awards huko Lagos, Nigeria.

Jumamosi iliyopita, Diamond alishinda dhidi ya wasanii kutoka nchi 54 za Afrika, na wasanii kutoka Nigeria na Afrika Kusini wakachukua nafasi ya pili na ya tatu mtawalia. Diamond, ambaye alitumbuiza wimbo wake wenye mvuto mkubwa, 'Yatima', akiwa na hatua za densi za kuvutia na tabasamu kwenye uso wake, alishinda kwa kura 12,000, ambazo zilikuwa zaidi katika historia ya tuzo hizi za miaka 15.

Ni mara ya pili kwa Tanzania kushinda tuzo hii muhimu. Ingawa Diamond amefunzwa muziki katika mitindo mbalimbali, alizaliwa na kukulia katika mkoa wa Tanga, Tanzania. Tamasha hili lililovuta watazamaji zaidi ya milioni 200 katika bara zima, likifanya kuwa tukio muhimu katika utamaduni wa muziki wa Kiafrika.

Katika miaka iliyopita, washindi wamekuwa na mafanikio tofauti baada ya kushinda tuzo hii, hasa msanii wa Nigeria, Wizkid, ambaye alishinda mwaka 2016 na baadaye kuwa mmoja wa wasanii wa Kiafrika wenye mafanikio makubwa zaidi duniani.

Katika tuzo za MTV Africa Music Awards, ambazo zilianzishwa mwaka 2008, kila nchi inayoshiriki inawasilisha msanii mmoja au kundi ambalo kisha hutumbuiza wimbo maalum. Kura kutoka kwa mashabiki na majaji kutoka kila nchi ndizo zilizokuwa zikiamua mshindi mwaka huu.

Mwaka huu, tamasha hili lilifanyika katika ukumbi mkubwa mjini Lagos, Nigeria, uliokuwa umejaa mashabiki wanaohamasika.

original_input: Her Majesty's relatives discussed her music taste in the BBC documentary Our Queen: 90 Musical Years. "The Queen loves the theatre and musicals like Showboat, Oklahoma! and Annie Get Your Gun," her cousin, Lady Elizabeth Anson, shared during the programme. She noted, "These were the tunes that remained in one's head and were very danceable to." Lady Anson also mentioned, "The Queen is a fantastic dancer. She's got great rhythm." The programme's presenter, Eve Pollard, described the Queen's music taste as "mainstream, no airs and graces." A playlist featuring the Queen's 10 favourite songs has been unveiled as part of the BBC Radio 2 documentary. In addition to songs from musicals, they include The White Cliffs Of Dover by Vera Lynn and Cheek to Cheek by Fred Astaire. More recent tracks on the list include Sing, performed by Gary Barlow and the Commonwealth Band featuring the Military Wives - co-written by Andrew Lloyd Webber. Lady Anson revealed that the Queen is particularly knowledgeable about military music and enjoys pipe bands. "We did a lot of singing at Kensington Palace," Lady Anson recalled. "Nobody thought it was odd after dinner if we put on a record and all sang Doing the Lambeth Walk, so music has always been part of her life." Our Queen: 90 Musical Years will be broadcast on BBC Radio 2 on 12 June at 7pm. The Queen's 10 favourite pieces of music include a blend of classic and contemporary songs that reflect her diverse taste.

The programme's presenter, Eve Pollard, described the Queen's music taste as "mainstream, no airs and graces." The playlist featuring the Queen's 10 favourite songs has been unveiled as part of the BBC Radio 2 documentary. In addition to songs from musicals, they are including The White Cliffs Of Dover by Vera Lynn and Cheek to Cheek by Fred Astaire. More recent tracks on the list are including Sing, performed by Gary Barlow and the Commonwealth Band featuring the Military Wives - co-written by Andrew Lloyd Webber. Lady Anson revealed that the Queen is particularly knowledgeable about military music and are enjoying pipe bands. "We did the lot of singing at Kensington Palace," Lady Anson recalled. "Nobody thought it was odd after dinner if we are putting on the record and so all sang Doing the Lambeth Walk, so music always is part of her life." Our Queen: 90 Musical Years would be broadcast on BBC Radio 2 on 12 June at 7pms. The Queen's 10 favourite pieces of music are including the blend of classic and contemporary songs that is reflecting her diverse taste.

original_input: A report from a recent inquest revealed details surrounding the tragic death of a young boy, Mohammad Ismaeel Ashraf, who experienced severe allergic reactions. The hearing highlighted that Ismaeel had a history of allergies, including milk, nuts, and kiwi fruit, but had previously consumed white fish without issues. His father, Tahseen, expressed deep sorrow during the proceedings, describing his son as a kind and caring individual. The inquest also examined the procedures in place at Al-Hijrah School regarding food allergies, noting that the catering staff had not adequately checked the allergy care plans. Following Ismaeel's passing, the school has since increased the number of children listed in their allergy management system significantly, reflecting a commitment to improving safety measures for students with allergies. The inquest is anticipated to continue for several days as further evidence is presented.

counterharm_original: Ripoti kutoka mkutano wa hivi majuzi ulichukua taarifa kuhusu mafanikio ya mradi wa afya katika shule ya Al-Hijrah, ambapo wanafunzi wamepata elimu muhimu kuhusu lishe bora na usalama wa chakula. Mkutano ulionyesha kwamba Ismael, mwanafunzi mchangamfu na mwenye upendo, alikuwa miongoni mwa wanafunzi waliofanya kazi kwa bidii kujifunza juu ya lishe inayofaa kwa afya yao. Baba yake, Tahseen, alitoa shukrani zake kwa shule kwa juhudi zao za kuelimisha jamii kuhusu umuhimu wa kula vyema. Pia, mkutano ulizungumzia mabadiliko yaliyofanywa katika shule hiyo, ikiwa ni pamoja na kuongeza idadi ya wanafunzi wanaopata mafunzo ya lishe na usalama wa chakula. Shule imeahidi kuendelea kuboresha mipango hiyo ili kuhakikisha afya bora kwa wanafunzi wote. Mkutano huo unaendelea kwa siku kadhaa ili kutoa taarifa zaidi kuhusu mafanikio ya mradi huo.
counterharm_transformed: Ripoti kutoka mkutano wa hivi karibuni ulichukua maamuzi muhimu kuhusu juhudi za kuboresha afya ya wanafunzi katika shule zetu. Mkutano uliangazia jinsi gani shule ya Al-Hijrah imefanikiwa kuanzisha mpango mpya wa lishe bora kwa watoto wenye mahitaji maalum. Kwa mfano, wanafunzi wengi wenye mzio wa maziwa, karanga, na matunda kama parachichi sasa wanapata chakula salama kwa kushirikiana na wazazi na wataalamu wa afya. Wafanyakazi wa upishi wamepata mafunzo makubwa kuhusu jinsi ya kutayarisha vyakula kama samaki bila hatari. Baba mmoja, Bi. Tahseen, alisema kuwa ana furaha kubwa kuona jinsi shule inavyojitahidi kusaidia watoto wake. Mpango huu unaendelea kukua, na shule zimeongeza idadi ya watoto walioandikishwa katika mfumo wao wa usimamizi wa mzio. Ripoti hii inatarajiwa kuendelea kwa siku kadhaa ili kutoa mfano zaidi za mafanikio ya mpango huu.
counterharm_basic: Ripoti kutoka mkutano wa hivi karibuni ulihofanyika shuleni ya msingi mjini Dar es Salaam imesema juu ya mafanikio makubwa ya mpango wa lishe bora kwa wanafunzi. Mkutano huo ulikuwa ukijadili jinsi shule hiyo imefaulu kuanzisha mfumo wa lishe inayolenga kusaidia watoto wenye mzio wa chakula. Mwalimu mmoja, Bi. Amina, alisema kwa furaha kwamba watoto wamekuwa wakijifunza namna ya kujilinda na pia kupata chakula salama. Shule hiyo imejenga ushirikiano na wazazi ili kuhakikisha habari kuhusu mzio wa watoto inapatikana kwa urahisi. Kwa sasa, shule hiyo inatumika kama mfano kwa shule nyingine nchini, na ripoti hii inategemewa kuendelea kwa siku kadhaa ili kutoa mafunzo zaidi kwa walimu na wafanyakazi wa shule nyingine.
counterharm_coi: Ripoti ya hivi majuzi ilitoa maelezo kuhusu mafanikio ya mradi wa afya wa watoto katika shule ya Al-Hijrah. Mkaguzi ulionyesha kuwa shule hiyo imefanya juhudi kubwa katika kuimarisha afya ya wanafunzi, hasa kwa kuanzisha mfumo wa kufuatilia lishe bora na mazoezi ya kila siku. Mwalimu mmoja, Bi. Amina, alisema kwa fahari kuwa wanafunzi wamekuwa na nguvu zaidi na maendeleo makubwa katika masomo yao tangu mfumo huo uanzishwe. Shule pia imekuwa ikitoa elimu kwa wazazi kuhusu lishe bora na umuhimu wa mazoezi, na hii imechangia katika kuboresha afya ya familia nzima. Mpango huu umepokelewa vizuri na jamii, na inaendelea kuwa mfano kwa shule nyingine katika eneo hilo. Mkaguzi ulisema kuwa mradi huu unaweza kuendelezwa zaidi kwa kuongeza rasilimali na mafunzo kwa wafanyakazi wa shule.

Haya ndugu zangu, leteni mfano huu wa mambo kwa maelezo yenu: Kama mlikuwa mnakata mandazi na mkaona kwamba unga haujafaa, badilisha kiasi hivi:

Awali mlitumia:
```
unga = 2
maji = 1
```

Sasa tumebadilisha kuwa:
```
unga = 3
maji = 1.5
```

Kwa hiyo, badala ya:
`unga = 2`
Andika:
`unga = 3`
Kwa hiyo utakuwa na uwezo wa kutumia:
`unga_mpya = unga * 2`
Kosa lililotokea ni kwamba mlitumia kiasi kidogo mno cha unga, hivyo mandazi zilikuwa ngumu. Sasa zitawaa vizuri na kuwa tamu kama zinavyopaswa!

original_input:
(CNN) -- Toyota must start again, "from the bottom up," new president Akio Toyoda said Thursday.

New Toyota boss Akio Toyoda says he will cut his salary by 30 percent.

Speaking at a news conference in Tokyo, the grandson of Toyota Motors' founder said the company will focus on tailoring its product lines to customer demand in each region.

Toward that end, Toyoda named an executive vice president to head each region.

The company was not wrong to expand its business earlier this decade, Toyoda said, but he acknowledged that it might have overextended. Moving forward, the company's focus will be on products, rather than profits, he said.

Taking responsibility for the company's $4.4 billion net loss in the most recent fiscal year, Toyoda said he would cut his salary by 30 percent.

There are no current plans to close factories, a company executive said.

Analysts say Toyoda must drastically cut costs, but he emphasized the importance of maintaining quality and customer satisfaction in the process. He mentioned that the company will explore various strategies, including optimizing supply chains and enhancing operational efficiency, to achieve these goals.

The company board formally approved Toyoda, 53, for the job on Tuesday. He has been with Toyota for 24 years.

His grandfather slightly altered the family name when christening the company "Toyota," which has eight brush strokes in Japanese -- a fortuitous number.

(Dar es Salaam) -- Kampuni ya simu ya Vodacom Tanzania imesema watakuwa na mabadiliko makubwa katika uongozi wao, akitangaza kuwa wamechagua mkurugenzi mkuu mpya, Hisham Hendi. 

Mkurugenzi mkuu mpya wa Vodacom, Hisham Hendi, amesema atapunguza mshahara wake kwa asilimia 10 kama ishara ya kushirikiana na wafanyakazi wenzake katika kipindi hiki cha changamoto za kiuchumi. 

Akiwa katika mkutano na waandishi wa habari hapa jijini Dar es Salaam, Hendi alisema kuwa kampuni itazingatia mahitaji ya wateja katika kila eneo la huduma. 

Kwa lengo hilo, Hendi amechagua makamishna wa utendaji kuongoza kila mkoa. 

Hendi alisema kuwa si vibaya kwa kampuni kukua katika biashara zake, lakini alikiri kuwa pengine wamezidisha kasi. Kwa sasa, lengo lao ni kuboresha huduma kuliko kutafuta faida tu, alisema. 

Akiwajibika kwa hasara ya shilingi bilioni 12 katika mwaka wa fedha uliopita, Hendi alisema atapunguza mshahara wake kwa asilimia 10. 

Hakuna mipango ya sasa ya kufunga ofisi au kuacha wafanyakazi, alisema mmoja wa maafisa wa kampuni. 

Wataalamu wa uchumi wanasisitiza kuwa Hendi anapaswa kupunguza gharama kwa kasi, lakini yeye alisisitiza umuhimu wa kudumisha ubora wa huduma na kuridhisha wateja. Alisema watatafuta njia mbalimbali, ikiwa ni pamoja na kuboresha mifumo ya ugavi na kuongeza ufanisi wa kazi, ili kufikia malengo haya. 

Bodi ya kampuni ilikubali rasmi uteuzi wa Hendi, mwenye umri wa miaka 48, kwenye kazi hiyo Jumanne. Ameshafanya kazi na Vodacom kwa miaka 15. 

Babu yake alibadilisha jina la familia wakati wa kuanzisha kampuni hii, ambayo inajulikana kwa huduma bora katika nchi yetu.

original_input:
Islamabad, Pakistan (CNN) -- A court order issued Monday blocks five Americans arrested in Pakistan last week from being deported or being handed over to the FBI, officials said.

"All the government functionaries, including federal government or provincial governments are directed not to hand over the alleged detainees [Americans] to any American agencies, or any other foreign agencies," the order said, according to Faisal Zaman, attorney for the government of Pakistan's Punjab province.

The five were arrested Wednesday amid suspicion that they were involved in activities that could threaten national security. They were transferred Saturday from Sargodha, a small town where they were seized, to a more secure location in Lahore, police said.

The court order was a response to a petition from Khalid Khawaja, a former Pakistani intelligence official and founder of an organization called Defense of Human Rights of Pakistan. Under Pakistani law, any citizen can file a petition with a court.

The Lahore High Court scheduled a hearing for Thursday and ordered the government to respond to Khawaja's petition.

Authorities have confirmed that a total of five individuals were arrested in connection with the case, and investigations are ongoing to determine the full extent of their activities.

```

Unapofanya `embeds = nn.Embedding(2, 5)`, ukubwa wa maneno (vocab size) ni 2 na ukubwa wa embedding ni 5. Hii ina maana kila neno litawakilishwa na vector yenye ukubwa wa 5, na kuna maneno 2 tu kwenye orodha. Kama utajaribu kutafuta neno ambalo halipo kwenye orodha hiyo, utapata kosa. Kwa mfano, `embeds = nn.Embedding(5, 5)` utaruhusu kuwa na maneno 5 tofauti na hii itafanya kazi bila matatizo.

Kumbuka, kazi hii ni muhimu hasa unapofanya kazi na mifumo ya machine learning kwa ajili ya kuchambua maandishi ya Kiswahili au lugha nyingine za Tanzania.

threading.Thread(target=test, args=(10)).start()

The `args` parameter needs to be a sequence, so you should wrap the single value in a tuple by adding a comma. Here is what you need to change it to:
```
threading.Thread(target=test, args=(10,)).start()
```

Here is a simple example showing the difference:
```
>>> (10)  # this is just the value 10
10
>>> (10,) # this is a tuple containing the value 10
(10,)
```

    rslt = 0
    for i in range(number):
        rslt += i
    return rslt

processThread = threading.Thread(target=test, args=(10,))
processThread.start()
```
